name: Insulinoma
description: Insulinoma is a tumour of the insulin-producing beta cells of the pancreas that secretes excess insulin, causing persistent hypoglycaemia. Episodes of weakness, collapse, and seizures result from the brain being starved of glucose.
treatment_overview: Surgical removal of visible tumours is the treatment of choice. Medical management with frequent small meals, prednisolone, and diazoxide helps stabilise blood glucose. Prognosis is guarded due to malignancy risk.
